QF.id: XVI Code of Conduct and Investor Protection_22

question.text: The IGRC (Investor Grievance Redressal Committee) at the exchange level typically resolves complaints within:

answer.text: The prescribed timeline set by the exchange — usually 15-30 days
No timeline — it takes as long as needed

explanation.text: **The Logic:** IGRC has prescribed resolution timelines (typically 15-30 days). If unresolved, investors can escalate to SCORES or arbitration. **The Trap:** Not one year (too long). There IS a timeline. Not same-day (that's unrealistic for most cases). **Cross-Exam Context:** Series VIII covers IGRC timelines. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** If IGRC doesn't resolve your complaint within the prescribed timeline, don't wait — escalate to SCORES immediately to ensure formal documentation and tracking.
